skill.name: llm-evaluation
skill.description: Design evaluation programs for LLM applications using automated metrics, human review, LLM judges, benchmarks, A/B tests, and regression checks.
skill.summary: Plan practical LLM evaluation workflows for model quality, safety, and release confidence.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command, weak cryptography, sensitive key, and network reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, metric terminology, and benign documentation text, with no executable scripts, prompt injection, or malicious intent found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution findings are Markdown code fences and Python examples, not Ruby shell execution. The weak cryptography, certificate/key, and network reconnaissance alerts map to ordinary evaluation text such as metrics, rating descriptions, and benchmark resource references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain documentation, Python examples, and ordinary English text. No executable skill code, shell invocation, credential access, or network scanning behavior is present.

content.value_statement: LLM teams need clear evidence before changing prompts, models, or retrieval systems. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design metric suites, review workflows, benchmarks, and regression checks.
actual_capabilities: Select automated metrics for generation, classification, and retrieval tasks.
Design pointwise and pairwise LLM-as-judge evaluation workflows.
Create human evaluation rubrics and annotation criteria.
Plan A/B tests with significance checks and effect size analysis.
Compare baseline and current results to detect regressions.
Structure benchmark runs and aggregate model performance results.
limitations: Referenced resource, asset, and script files are not included in this package.
Code examples are illustrative and may need current library changes.
It does not validate datasets for privacy, bias, or contamination.
LLM judges still require calibration against human review.

use_cases.title: Build Release Gates
use_cases.description: Create evaluation checks that compare new model or prompt versions against a baseline before deployment.

use_cases.title: Compare Product Variants
use_cases.description: Measure response quality across prompts, models, or retrieval settings using metrics and human feedback.

use_cases.title: Investigate Quality Regressions
use_cases.description: Identify drops in accuracy, relevance, safety, or helpfulness after a system change.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Evaluation Metrics
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new LLM feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose evaluation metrics for an LLM feature that does [task]. Include automated metrics, human review dimensions, and pass criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Test Set Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a test set plan for my LLM application. Cover example categories, edge cases, expected outputs, and how to avoid data contamination.

prompt_templates.title: Design LLM Judge Rubrics
prompt_templates.scenario: Using a stronger model to score outputs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an LLM-as-judge rubric for comparing two responses. Include scoring dimensions, bias controls, and validation against human ratings.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Regression Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a regression testing strategy for our LLM system. Include baseline tracking, thresholds, statistical analysis, failure triage, and release gates.

output_examples.input: Evaluate a customer support chatbot before launch.
output_examples.output: A practical plan with accuracy, relevance, safety, and helpfulness metrics, a representative test set, human review criteria, and launch thresholds.

output_examples.input: Compare two retrieval prompts for a RAG workflow.
output: Recommendation for retrieval and answer quality metrics.
Pairwise judge rubric for comparing responses.
A/B test guidance with baseline and significance checks.

output_examples.input: Find why model quality dropped after a prompt update.
output_examples.output: A regression analysis approach that compares baseline results, identifies affected metric groups, reviews failed examples, and suggests targeted fixes.

best_practices: Use multiple metrics so one score does not hide important failures.
Keep evaluation data representative, versioned, and separate from training data.
Validate automated and LLM-judge results with periodic human review.
anti_patterns: Relying on a single metric for broad quality decisions.
Changing prompts without a stable baseline and test set.
Using LLM judges without calibration or bias checks.

faq.question: What kinds of LLM systems can this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: It can guide evaluation for chatbots, RAG systems, classification flows, summarizers, and other text generation applications.

faq.question: Does it run evaluations automatically?
faq.answer: No. The package contains guidance and examples, but the referenced runner scripts are not included.

faq.question: Can it compare models or prompts?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains pairwise comparison, A/B testing, benchmarks, and baseline tracking.

faq.question: Does it replace human evaluators?
faq.answer: No. It recommends combining automated metrics, LLM judges, and human review for stronger evidence.

faq.question: Can it help with safety evaluation?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes safety as a review dimension and discusses toxicity and groundedness checks.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare representative examples, expected behavior, baseline results, and the business goal for each metric.
